title: hieracium aphyllum subsp. axiflorum
intro: Hieracium aphyllum subsp. axiflorum, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion-like flowering plants in the sunflower family. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and stems, which contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
